buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)

window = 60

# Rolling stats using only past data
rolling_mean = df['close'].rolling(window=window).mean()
rolling_std = df['close'].rolling(window=window).std()

# Lag by 1 to avoid lookahead
df['zscore'] = (df['close'] - rolling_mean.shift(1)) / rolling_std.shift(1)

# Signal: buy when zscore < -1, sell when zscore > 1
df['signal'] = 0
df.loc[df['zscore'] < -1, 'signal'] = 1
df.loc[df['zscore'] > 1, 'signal'] = -1

# Lag signal by 1 before multiplying by returns
returns = df['close'].pct_change()
df['strategy'] = df['signal'].shift(1) * returns
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)

window = 60

rolling_mean = df['close'].rolling(window=window).mean()
rolling_std = df['close'].rolling(window=window).std()

df['zscore'] = (df['close'] - rolling_mean.shift(1)) / rolling_std.shift(1)

df['signal'] = 0
df.loc[df['zscore'] < -1, 'signal'] = 1
df.loc[df['zscore'] > 1, 'signal'] = -1

returns = df['close'].pct_change()
df['strategy'] = df['signal'].shift(1) * returns
description: Clean example — rolling z-score mean reversion with properly lagged stats and signal.
explanation: Rolling mean and std are both shifted by 1 before computing z-score. Signal is also shifted by 1 before multiplying by returns. Double lagging ensures no lookahead at any step.
